mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on future earnings and supports equity multiples, so sustained declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is trending down for at least 3 consecutive monthly observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates and the policy rate is still low, the market anticipates future tightening and a higher discount rate, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign of the return difference versus untriggered periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and slower final demand, which should reduce cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not lower after monthly UNRATE increases than after flat or falling UNRATE changes across walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad demand momentum, improving revenue growth expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with both RSAFS and HOUST rising fail to produce higher 60-day forward SPX returns than periods where only one or neither rises.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production with stable or falling unemployment reflects improving real activity and capacity utilization, which should lift equity earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while UNRATE does not increase by more than 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the conditional 20-day SPX return after the trigger is not positive relative to baseline or if the interaction adds no incremental explanatory power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts captures a tightening credit and construction slowdown that feeds into weaker growth expectations, making near-term S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% from the prior monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the median return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, even small deterioration in payroll growth can signal an approaching earnings slowdown, so a payroll drop with UNRATE already low should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE is below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered payroll declines under low unemployment do not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the sign is positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate regime compresses valuation multiples and only becomes strongly bearish when inflation is still elevated, so elevated FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPI should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative in most triggered periods, or if high-rate high-inflation periods do not underperform other regimes.
